subject_summary: Support ticket created for a user account that no longer exists in the system. Account was deleted 3 days ago following a user-initiated closure request. The ticket ID is valid but references a deleted entity.
reasoning: Skip: subject references a deleted account. DataSourceTool returned not_found. No actionable subject exists — recording and discarding this cycle.

subject_summary: DevOps alert received with a malformed payload. Required fields 'service_name' and 'alert_type' are missing. The alert ID exists in the monitoring system but the record is corrupted. Cannot determine what the alert is about.
reasoning: Skip: alert payload is corrupted and missing required fields. Cannot analyse an event with no service name or alert type. Recording skip and flagging monitoring system for data integrity investigation.

subject_summary: Research document URL returned HTTP 404. The document was queued from an RSS feed item 2 hours ago but the source URL no longer resolves. The document may have been retracted or the URL may have been published in error.
reasoning: Skip: source URL returns 404 — document does not exist at the configured location. DataSourceTool raised SubjectNotFound. Recording skip and flagging the feed source for dead-link review.

subject_summary: Moderation queue item references a post ID that was deleted by the user before the moderation cycle ran. The post content is no longer available. The flag exists in the moderation queue but there is nothing to moderate.
reasoning: Skip: post was deleted by the user before moderation could run. No content exists to review. Recording skip — the moderation queue entry will be cleaned up automatically.

subject_summary: Pipeline bot received a data file with a completely unrecognised format. File extension is .dat but content is binary with no identifiable schema or magic bytes matching any known format. File cannot be parsed by any configured parser.
reasoning: Skip: binary file with unrecognised format — no parser can process this input. Recording skip and quarantining the file for manual investigation by the data engineering team.

subject_summary: Support ticket submitted via the API with all text fields populated with placeholder values: subject='test', body='test test test', customer_id='12345'. Account ID 12345 does not exist. This is clearly a test submission that reached production.
reasoning: Skip: test submission with placeholder values that reached production. Customer ID does not exist. Recording and discarding — flagging API intake for input validation to prevent test data from entering the production queue.

subject_summary: Research bot queued a document that, on retrieval, turns out to be a duplicate of a document processed and published 4 hours ago. Content hash matches exactly. Same source, same publication timestamp, same author. No new information.
reasoning: Skip: exact content hash duplicate of a document already processed this cycle. Publishing a duplicate would corrupt the output store. Recording skip and flagging the source feed for deduplication.

subject_summary: DevOps alert references a service named 'legacy-worker-v1' that was decommissioned 6 months ago. The alert was triggered by a monitoring rule that was not cleaned up after decommission. The service, its infrastructure, and its metrics no longer exist.
reasoning: Skip: alert for a decommissioned service that no longer exists. Cannot remediate infrastructure that has been removed. Recording skip and flagging the monitoring rule for cleanup.

subject_summary: Pipeline bot received a data file that fails checksum validation. The SHA-256 hash in the manifest does not match the hash of the received file. File may have been corrupted in transit or the manifest may be for a different file version.
reasoning: Skip: checksum validation failed — file integrity cannot be guaranteed. Loading corrupt data would risk downstream data quality. Recording skip and requesting re-delivery from the source system.

subject_summary: Support ticket created by an automated system with subject='null' and body containing only a JSON serialisation error message: 'TypeError: Cannot read property id of undefined'. The ticket was generated by a broken webhook integration, not a real user issue.
reasoning: Skip: ticket is a serialisation error from a broken webhook, not a user issue. Subject content is a stack trace with no actionable information. Recording skip and alerting the integration owner to fix the webhook handler.

subject_summary: Moderation queue received a report for a comment that was already actioned in a previous cycle — the comment was removed 2 cycles ago for a separate policy violation. The current report flag is stale and references content that no longer exists.
reasoning: Skip: report references already-removed content. The comment was actioned in a prior cycle. No moderation action is needed or possible. Recording skip — stale report will be cleared from the queue.

subject_summary: Research bot attempted to retrieve a paywalled article. The source URL returns HTTP 200 with a login redirect page, not the article content. DataSourceTool received HTML login form instead of document text. No substantive content is available.
reasoning: Skip: source URL returns a login redirect — no article content was retrieved. Recording skip and removing this source domain from the approved feed list until access credentials are configured.

subject_summary: Pipeline bot received an empty file — file size is 0 bytes. The manifest declares 50,000 expected rows. The sending system may have experienced an export failure and sent an empty file placeholder.
reasoning: Skip: received an empty file (0 bytes) that should contain 50,000 rows. This is a sender-side export failure. Recording skip and alerting the data provider to re-send the file.

subject_summary: Support ticket arrived with a customer ID that belongs to a test account created during QA. The test account was not cleaned up from the production database. The reported issue is a test scenario, not a real customer problem.
reasoning: Skip: ticket is from a QA test account that was not cleaned up from production. Not a real customer issue. Recording skip and flagging the QA team to remove test accounts from the production environment.

subject_summary: DevOps alert triggered by a synthetic monitoring probe that is intentionally designed to fail as part of a canary alerting test. The alert payload contains the field 'synthetic_test=true'. No real service is affected.
reasoning: Skip: alert is a synthetic monitoring test (synthetic_test=true flag present). No real service is affected. Recording skip — the monitoring pipeline is correctly routing synthetic alerts to the production queue, which should be fixed.
